entries.polished_md: - Hail Amulius, and continue speaking with him to get the option to enter Bloodskull Valley: Maulic's Stronghold.
- Speak with this NPC to spawn [K'Dal the Deceiver](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/K'Dal_the_Deceiver).

## Pull

It is recommended that you pull Darathar into the water, and have **casters** positioned at maximum range to avoid incurable trauma damage.

## Mechanics

### Self-Heal Thresholds

He will cast a raid-wide stun and heals for **25% of his maximum health** three times during the fight, at **75%**, **50%** and **25%** health:

- At **75%** health, he will heal to full health.
- At **50%** he heals to **75%** health.
- At **25%** he heals back to **50%** health.

After that point, he will no longer heal himself.

### Flame of Ages

His special attack, [Flame of Ages](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Flame_of_Ages), is an **elemental area-of-effect** spell which does a high amount of direct damage and has a damage-over-time component. He casts this approximately every **50 seconds**.

> Each member should have **elemental cure potions** to ensure they can cure themselves in case a healer is unable to cure them immediately.

## Notes

The self-heals only occur **once per instance** at each health threshold, **not per encounter**. For example, if you get Darathar below 50% and then the raid wipes, Darathar will only heal once during subsequent fights. If the raid wipes after taking Darathar to 25% health, he will not heal and stun again in that instance.

## Mechanics

- Has **four adds** to start
- He will periodically call in **2–3 54^^^ adds**, after which he will drop a **~2000hp AoE**
- If the adds reach the named, they will **explode for ~3k Focus damage per add**

## Mechanics

- **Treyloth D'Kulvith** will mem wipe at **75%**, **50%**, and **25%** health.
- You need to fight between **10m and 5m** of him to avoid both of his AoEs.
- He also places an **arcane detrimental** on the raid which causes your spells to reflect.
- The 'Disciples of Innoruuk' function as the priest guild on Neriak.
Spawns with **2 adds**, a **martial advisor** and a **magical advisor**.

Comes with a bunch of [a knotted guardian](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_knotted_guardian) adds. The adds are easy to kill and don't do much dps. If you have a lot of **AoE**, just focus on the named. Adds had a total of **790,000 HP**. Make sure you let Wuoshi pass by so she doesn't aggro your raid if it takes too long to burn Treah down.

entries.zone_name: The Eternal Gorge: A Daring Confrontation

entries.polished_md:
## Encounter

Comes grouped with the following as an adventure party that your raid must beat:

- [Blorgok the Brutal](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Blorgok_the_Brutal) — **Fighter**
- [Meldrath Kloktik](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Meldrath_Kloktik) — **Mage**
- 2 [Froglok Mercenaries](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/a_froglok_mercenary) — **Fighters**
- [Ludmila Kystov](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Ludmila_Kystov) — **Scout**

## Mechanics

- **Judgement of the Faceless**: Disease-based AoE Fear & DoT (every **45s**) — does more damage the closer you are.

## Strategy

- **MT** pulls to the far left corner with **Sanctuary** up.
- Everyone else stays outside of the room until **Judgement of the Faceless** hits, then rush in.
- Joust all the way out of the room every **45s** before Judgement hits, then back in after.

### Adds

- Adds can be **mezzed**, or burn them down first.

## Overview

This is a **burn fight**.

## Mechanics

### Sacs

Tairiza spawns **"sacs"** approximately every **30 seconds**. A sac will land on a random person in the raid, **incapacitating** them and **killing them over time**.

### 40% Spider Adds

At approximately **40%** she spawns a group of spiders.

> **NOTE**: The spider adds are **immune to all taunts**.

## Positioning

- Have **ranged DPS** and **healers** to the left or right of the door with a **Ranged DPS main assist** calling the sacs and making the ranged dps cycle between killing the sacs then immediately back on the named.
- **Melee DPS** stands directly under the Widow Mistress, targets the mob, and burns it down.
- If a sac spawns on melee DPS or the **Main Tank**, melee DPS can **TAB**, burn the sac down, then resume on the named.

## Add Phase

- Once the spiders pop, ignore the sacs and focus the burn on the mob.
- A **zerker** or strong **AE'ing offtank** should pick up as many spiders as possible.
- Alternatively, multiple **Enchanters** can **mezz** them.
- Either way, **burn Tairiza as fast as possible** once the adds start.
- Save big spells/CAs with long recast timers for that final push.

## Finish

Once the spiders pop, everyone targets the named directly and burns. **AoEs** will clear a lot of the spiders; finish off any remaining after the named dies and loot the metal chest.

- To disable her charm ability, destroy the two statues in [The Garden of Purity](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Garden_of_Purity) and [The Garden of Clarity](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/The_Garden_of_Clarity).

## Zone Layout

- When you enter, you are in a zone in/zone out room which leads to gigantic stairs. You must use the side area of the stairs to walk up into the main lair.
- When you first enter the zone, Trakanon will greet you from far above the platform, then fly down. At this point you can engage Trakanon.

## Chelsith Stone & The Orb

- You must have a **chelsith stone** with you to have any chance to succeed. It drops in the leviathan encounter in-game.
- Once the fight begins, the holder of a chelsith stone can touch the **orb altar** on the far side of the platform to energize it. Once energized, if you stay within **14m** of the energized orb structure, you are protected from many of Trakanon's more powerful abilities.
- The key to winning this encounter is to fight within **14m** of the activated orb on the far side of the platform. Trakanon's **arcane maelstrom** breath weapon is far too lethal outside this protective area. In addition, Trakanon's knockback abilities are negated (all but one) when you are close to this orb object if it is energized.

## Mechanics

### Wing Buffet & Tail Swipe

Trakanon is a **level 87 warlock**. He will periodically cast **Wing Buffet** and **Tail Swipe**.

- **Wing Buffet** will **STUN** everyone in front of him for **2.5 seconds**. It will also **KNOCK** everyone back UNLESS they are within **14m** of the energized orb.
- **Tail Swipe** does some damage, and stuns players for about **3 seconds**. It will also **KNOCK BACK** any players that are NOT within **14m** of the energized orb.

### Immunities & Attack Profile

Trakanon is immune to all control spells, and will double attack on melee **10%** more than normal.

### Arcane Aura of the Wyrmlord / Pestilential Pause

Trakanon has **Arcane Aura of the Wyrmlord** on him. On take damage **10%** of the time, players will be hit with **Pestilential Pause**. This does a slight amount of damage and for the next **10 seconds**, all the player's spells take much longer to cast and are basically at half effectiveness (heals and damage are half as effective as normal). Attack speed is reduced considerably during this time. This effect is **not dispellable**. Also, **2%** of the time Trakanon is hit with a hostile spell, he will heal himself for **2%**.

### Prophetic Scrutiny

Trakanon will keep **PROPHETIC SCRUTINY** on one player at all times. With this effect on you, any spells you cast are interrupted, if the spell is interruptable. Every time you start to cast a spell you will be hit with **PROPHETIC BOLT**, which deals a small amount of damage and interrupts the spell. This should be active on one player in the raid at all times.

### Prescient Gaze

Trakanon will keep **Prescient Gaze** on all players at all times. It should never be off you for more than a few seconds even if you die and are rezzed back in. It causes all players to mana burn, using health whenever they use mana. It also causes players to have **prescient intervention** cast on them **8%** of the time they try to cast a beneficial spell. Prescient intervention interrupts the spell, stifles the player for **2 seconds**, and does a small amount of damage to the player's mana pool.

### Mark of Repulsion / Static Repulsion

One player will generally have **MARK OF REPULSION** on them. This spell will go off at some random time, never less than **30 secs** after it was placed on you. It has an hourglass icon.

> When it goes off, that player will be hit with **static repulsion**, a targeted AoE that WILL knock back all players within **22m** of the person who had Mark of Repulsion on them. You are **NEVER** protected from this knockback, even if near the orb. You must move away from your raid party so as not to endanger them with your Mark of Repulsion/Static Repulsion combo.

### Arcane Maelstrom

**3.5 seconds AFTER** static repulsion goes off, Trakanon will cast **Arcane Maelstrom**. A powerful AoE that does heat damage, damages a random item in everyone's inventory, and does massive **MAGIC BASED** health and mana damage UNLESS you are close to the energized orb. You avoid MAGIC BASED MANA AND HEALTH DAMAGE only if you are near the energized orb.

### Fetid Cloud & Toxic Tempest

Trakanon will periodically cast **Fetid Cloud** and **Toxic Tempest** as well.

- **Fetid Cloud** does disease based health damage — the closer you are to Trak, the more disease damage you take — and slows your attack speed. If you have **Toxic Tempest** still on you when Fetid Cloud hits you, you will take **ADDITIONAL MASSIVE poison based health damage**.
- **Toxic Tempest** works similarly to Fetid Cloud — range-relative, poison based health damage to all players. If you have **Fetid Cloud** on you when Toxic Tempest hits you, you will take additional **MASSIVE disease damage** as well. Toxic Tempest also slows your movement rate.

## Enrage / Soft Timer

**800 seconds** after the raid fight begins, the platform you fight on begins to lower into the lava. This takes about **20-30 secs**, after which you will be forced to fight Trakanon in the lava.

- If you hop away from the platform, you won't be protected by the orb.
- If you stay near the orb, you will be covered in lava.

You have only about **820 secs** to win this fight.

If Trakanon dies, the platform will rise. If Trakanon resets, the platform will rise.

## Pull & Positioning

- Have your **Main Tank** pull this mob toward the **wall behind him**.
- Have the **MT group** stay tight as **LoS issues** will come up from time to time.
- Rest of the raid will be assisting the **Off Tank**.

## Adds

- At around **75%** or so, a message will pop up warning you that a mob will spawn — a **heroic ^^^ add** which has a pretty nasty AE (**hits harder than the main boss**).
- Have your **OT group** meet the add and be careful that they turn it **away from the raid** or else it means a wipe.
- At this time you will notice that a **targetable but non-killable baby dragon** will be running to groups of eggs. This mob will spawn three more adds (down arrows), but they still can wipe you fairly quickly.

## Rotation

Once those are taken care of, get back on named until the next message about an add incoming. Repeat until dead.

## Overview

In the same group as [Shade of Khalan Dar](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Shade_of_Khalan_Dar).

There are **two methods** of pulling and killing Phara Dar. When you approach Phara Dar's room you will notice the [Shade of Khalan Dar](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Shade_of_Khalan_Dar) in the center and a ramp leading up the left side which takes you to Phara Dar. Upon pulling Phara Dar, the Shade of Khalan Dar will immediately fly to wherever Phara Dar is located and assist in the battle. It is imperative to have a **balanced Main and Off Tank group** for this fight as you will need to kill both of these named mobs at once.

## Method 1 — Split at the Ramp

Send two groups (**Main** and an assisting group) up the ramp to Phara Dar and fight the **Shade of Khalan Dar** at the very bottom of the ramp with their back to the wall.

> The drawback: even with snaps and aggro lock, the **Shade of Khalan Dar will still fly up to Phara Dar 2/3 of the time** and have to be dragged back down the ramp to the Off Tank and their assisting group. This can result in a **wipe of the MT group** and a fight reset.

Once the OT group has killed the [Shade of Khalan Dar](https://eq2.fandom.com/wiki/Shade_of_Khalan_Dar) at the bottom of the ramp, they should run up and assist in DPS on Phara Dar.

## Method 2 — Hallway Pull (easier)

Pull Phara Dar and subsequently the Shade of Khalan Dar into the hallway that leads to the main room.

- The **MT group** and their assisting party should be located closest to the room entrance.
- The **OT group** and their assisting party should be located approximately **40 metres up the stairs**.
- Positioning **Phara Dar on the bottom far right** and the **Shade of Khalan Dar on the upper far left** corners of the staircase makes it easier for the OT and assisting group to switch over to Phara Dar once the shade is dead.

## Notes

> Please note: The **Shade of Khalan Dar will heal itself to full once during the fight** — this is not a bug.

## Overview

This mob is a relatively simple **tank and spank** encounter.

## Mechanics

- There is a **small arcane AOE** that can be easily avoided by standing at the edge of the room.
- You will also find **floating balls of lightning**. These have a **knockback** and can cause your spells to interrupt.

## Strategy

- The best method of avoiding the lightning balls is to have the **ranged DPS** stand at the very edge of the room.
  - You will notice that the texture of the floor is two different colors — the balls will not affect you if you stand on the **lighter tile**.
- **Healers** move in to attend to tanks and melee DPS as needed.
